tweets.full_text:
ちょっと、Claude で確認したいことがあったので、久しぶりにアクセスしたら、Opus は無料アカウントでは使えないと言う。

なるほどそれはそうだ。

ところで、Claude Codeのエラー関連で勝手に購入された$15 がアカウントにあるはずだが、ちょっと使えないか知らんと調べてみたけど、これは、どうやら、塩漬ってるらしい。無料アカウンになったら、手が付けられない物となってしまっているらしい。。。怒。

なんていうか、Claudeって、触るたびに血圧が上がるプロダクトなのだが、なんでみんな平安と使ってられるのか。。。

tweets.full_text:
やっぱりClaude Codeはすごい。

1600件以上あるイベント情報の中から、日々の行動履歴、趣味嗜好、過去の投稿内容、事業内容まで踏まえて、参加すべきイベントを選定。

さらに時間・場所・移動効率まで考慮して、最適なスケジュールを自動で組んでくれる。

しかもイベントの申し込みまで実行。

APIが用意されていないサイトでも、Claude_in_Chromeでブラウザを操作して完了させる。

もはや秘書というより、自分専属のエージェント。

tweets.full_text:
【速報】
海外でClaude Codeの「企業実例ゼロ」が逆にチャンスに見える理由

注目ポイントはここ
・X上に企業名・成果数値付き投稿はまだなし
・でもAutoモードやプラグイン自動読み込みが高評価
・Worktree管理強化で大規模長時間開発に最適化進む
・安全性重視のAnthropicモデルで安定稼働が支持されている

つまり
具体事例が表に出ていない今こそ、内部機能の充実が進み、企業導入の土台が静かに固まっている段階。

これまでは
「実績・数字の見える化が導入の鍵」
今は
「安定と拡張性を優先し信頼構築中」

その感覚、かなり正常です。

海外の大手やスタートアップも今は見守りフェーズ。
このタイミングでAutoモードをBedrockやVertex環境で試しつつ、プラグイン開発に挑戦してみてください。
表に出る「爆速成功」より前の、着実な基盤構築期を味わえる貴重なフェーズです。

quoted_status.full_text:
Anthropicは時価総額やMythosの話題ではイケイケ状態に見えるんですが、実はMythosリリース以降、ユーザーが使えるものではClaude Opus4.7以降、ずっとやらかしっぱなしで、性能も上がっているのか下がっているのかよくわからない、あと突然壊れるという意味不明な状況。
恐らく計算リソース投資が間に合ってなかったのと、Opus4.7以降は事後学習の失敗。あとはやや推測ですが、内部で持っているモデルの評価手法の問題。この辺はOpenAIの方が強かった。
Opus4.7以降は、来たるMythosレベルモデルのリリースのために、事後学習やMythosからの蒸留をしつつ、いかに脆弱性発見能力などを抑えて能力を維持するかのバランス取りをやっていると思いますが、これが難航しているのが現状なのでは。

user.description: PCゲーマー ラッパー 里の民🍃🥜 Claude Codeに出会ってバイブコーディングを始めました フロウに乗ってコード吐く毎日 好きなお寿司はアボカドサーモンです🍣

tweets.full_text:
【逆説】
コードを書かなくなるほど、プログラマーは自由になる

・Claude Codeのプラグインはもうマーケットを離れた
・`.claude/skills`に置くだけで即使える拡張性
・Gitワークツリーも自在に切り替えて管理できる

つまり
手を動かさずに環境を操ることが、創造の本質になった

これまでは
手でコードを書き、ファイルを管理し
↓
今は
思考と設計を磨き、AIに任せる

この変化に戸惑う人も多いはず
でもその感覚、かなり正常です

だからこそ今、手を動かすことの意味を問い直す時代が来ている

後で追うより、今この変化を感じておいた方がいいと思う

tweets.full_text:
Most engineers use Claude Code at max 10%.
The top 1% use these 12 features.
.
.
That's the difference between:
→ asking AI questions
and
→ building with AI.

☑ 1. CLAUDE.md
One file.
Your rules, architecture, stack, and commands.
Claude remembers the project from day one.

☑ 2. Permissions
Whitelist what AI can touch.
Block what it can't.
Control beats chaos.

☑ 3. Plan Mode
Review the plan before execution.
Approve first.
Code second.

☑ 4. Checkpoints
Create restore points.
Break something?
Rollback instantly.

☑ 5. Skills
Turn great prompts into reusable workflows.
Build once.
Reuse forever.

☑ 6. Hooks
Automate actions before and after tool use.
Claude becomes infrastructure.

☑ 7. MCP
Connect Claude to databases, APIs, and internal tools.
AI + tools = leverage.

☑ 8. Plugins
Extend capabilities with Docker, Pytest, and custom integrations.
Small advantages compound.

☑ 9. Context
Most people give AI tasks.
Top engineers give AI context.
Files. History. Rules. Tools.

☑ 10. Slash Commands
Create shortcuts like:
/review
Save hundreds of repeated prompts.

☑ 11. Compaction
Compress long conversations.
Keep the signal.
Drop the noise.

☑ 12. Subagents
Run research, coding, testing, and documentation in parallel.

AI starts feeling like a team.

Most engineers think AI is about prompts.

It's not.
It's about systems.

Learn these 12 features from best resources like : 
• https://t.co/y9qd5NJKkb → Learn the fundamentals fast.
• ByteByteGo → Understand how modern AI systems work.
• https://t.co/zaEIgKQ8UN → Master practical AI skills and workflows.
• Hugging Face → Build with real

and you'll be dramatically more productive than the average engineer in 2026.

♻️ Repost to help another engineer stop using Claude Code like ChatGPT.

THIS DEVELOPER SPENT $0 ON CLAUDE CODE TOKENS TODAY AND HEADROOM SAVED 3,376,624 OF THEM WHILE HE WAS CODING

tweets.full_text:
Claude Code + Higgsfield MPC = $12K Cinematic Websites.

I built a scroll-animated cinematic site in 90 min. No designer. No budget. No code skills.

Here's the exact playbook 👇

1/ Connect Higgsfield to Claude (30 sec) 

Settings → Connectors → Customize → "+" → name it "Higgsfield", paste the URL, authorize. 

2/ Let Claude build the brand

One prompt: "Landing page, one job - newsletter signups. 

Clean, big text, modern AI brand, coral accent." 5 min later: colors, font, logo draft. Done.

3/ Upgrade visuals with Higgsfield 

Feed the logo + hero image to Higgsfield: depth, cinematic lighting, premium look → drops straight into your project folder. 

4/ Add motion Static is half the job. 

Higgsfield + Cinema Studio = real camera moves. 

Generate 4 clips in one style to tell a story as you scroll.

5/ Ship the page 

Hand it back to Claude: "Single page, this brand, hero video, scroll-triggered playback." 

Claude wires it with WebGL + Three.js - video in 3D, buttery smooth.

6/ The last 10% is human Tools get you 90%. You pick which shots stay and which get cut.

90 min. $0 on designers. Setup takes 30 seconds. Go build with

tweets.full_text:
Uber's COO just admitted he can't prove their AI spend is working.

Here's what's wild about that.

Uber gave Claude Code to 5,000 engineers in December. 

Adoption exploded.

They built a leaderboard ranking teams by AI usage. Per-engineer API costs hit $500-$2,000 a month.

The full year AI budget was gone by April.

$951 million in R&D just in Q1 2026. 11% of backend code now written entirely by AI agents.

And Andrew Macdonald went on a podcast and said he cannot draw a clear line between any of that spending and better products for riders.

That's not a small company hedging. That's one of the biggest AI customers on the planet saying the ROI is genuinely unclear.

Gartner says companies will spend $207 billion on AI agents this year.

How many of those budgets will get the same answer when someone asks if it's working?

tweets.full_text:
New Claude Code hack:
Use /goal and then ask it to use as many parallel agents as possible all connected to its Chrome Extension MCP to test out every feature, click every button and all the frontend routes in all the different screen sizes. And then use voice to transcribe 30+ different things you want it to do. Ask it to run for hours and then do test and research extensively. Make sure it follows the design rulebook. After that have it auto commit and push to the remote preview branch so that Github Workflow auto deploys this preview app and you can test the app running from the cloud right after that. And then make sure the agent you're chatting with always delegate tasks to subagents so as to not waste its context window. It's like a coworker you're working with.

user.name: Shubh Thorat
user.description: FAANG engineer. Northeastern CS. Building Claude Code plugins. Advising AI startups. Ship fast, iterate faster.

tweets.full_text:
I found a GitHub repo that builds your entire Claude Code agent team from one sentence.

Not a template. A skill that writes other skills.

It's called Harness by revfactory. 3.6K stars. 539 forks.

Here's what that actually costs you today without it:

One agent file. One skill file. One domain at a time. You're configuring architecture before you've written a single line of real work. Most people spend an afternoon on setup that should take two minutes.

Harness runs a 6-phase pipeline from one prompt:

→ Reads your codebase and identifies what kind of work needs doing
→ Picks from 6 orchestration patterns: Pipeline, Fan-out, Expert Pool, Producer-Reviewer, Supervisor, Hierarchical Delegation
→ Generates every agent definition your project needs
→ Writes the skill files each agent uses
→ Validates the whole setup before touching your project

Output lands in .claude/agents/ and .claude/skills/ automatically. Nothing to wire. Nothing to copy from a tutorial at 11pm.

The companion repo, harness-100, adds 100 pre-built teams across software, content, legal, health, data, and business domains. Copy one folder. You're running a specialist team in under a minute.

I keep thinking about the fork count. 539 forks on 3.6K stars is not a casual bookmark. People are actually building on top of this.

MIT License. 100% Open Source.
